title: TypeScript package docs scope
summary: This file is the package-local documentation policy for @graphrefly/ts. It does not define the shared public website, shared protocol docs, Python docs, Rust docs, or the GraphReFly blog.
rules: The language-neutral authority lives in ~/src/graphrefly.
The shared graphrefly.dev website shell and public documentation architecture are owned by ~/src/graphrefly under D563.
This repo owns TypeScript package documentation inputs and automation only.

title: Authority order
rules: Protocol rules come from ~/src/graphrefly/spec/rules.jsonl.
Design locks and sequencing come from ~/src/graphrefly/decisions/decisions.jsonl and ~/src/graphrefly/plan/*.jsonl.
The public TypeScript package surface comes from packages/ts/package.json exports and packages/ts/tsup.config.ts entries.
Generated TypeScript API docs come from JSDoc on exported TypeScript APIs.
Runnable package-local examples and demos live in examples/ and demos/.
README, package release notes, and package-local metadata own install, quick start, and release-facing package guidance.

title: Repository boundary
rules: ~/src/graphrefly owns graphrefly.dev shared public website shell, shared guide records, public blog storage/rendering, and internal dashboard/control views.
~/src/graphrefly-ts owns @graphrefly/ts source, package exports, TypeScript JSDoc, package-local API reference generation, examples, demos, install notes, release notes, and docs automation scripts.
This repo must not hand-maintain mirrors of shared docs, protocol pages, public blog posts, Python docs, Rust docs, or the full internal dashboard.

title: Automation policy
rules: All TypeScript package docs must be mechanically reproducible.
API reference pages are generated from packages/ts exports plus JSDoc.
API sidebars and public symbol inventories are generated from the same export source.
Missing-doc reports are generated, not curated by hand.
Generated docs must have a check mode that fails when outputs are stale.
Manual package docs are allowed only when intentionally small source documents such as this policy, package README material, release notes, and explanatory example READMEs.

title: Old website migration
rules: The existing website/ directory is the old TypeScript-hosted public website plus a useful TypeScript API generation pipeline.
The public website part should be deleted or migrated out of this repo under D563.
The TypeScript API docs automation must survive in a package-local home.

Delete or migrate public homepage, shared public shell, blog content, shared protocol/spec pages, shared guide pages, comparisons, shared recipes, and language-neutral public docs out of graphrefly-ts.

Keep, move, or rebuild the TypeScript API docs generator, generated API output target, missing-doc checks, stale-output checks, example/demo metadata, and package-local docs artifacts consumed by graphrefly.dev/ts.

title: Blog boundary
rules: The GraphReFly blog belongs in ~/src/graphrefly, not graphrefly-ts.
Blog posts are public narrative for the shared project, not TypeScript package API docs.
Old blog posts under the TypeScript website are migration/reference material only.
Rebuild or curate blog posts in ~/src/graphrefly with explicit source refs to decisions, rules, guides, or package-local examples when needed.

title: TypeScript public surface JSDoc
optional: @seeAlso for related APIs or package examples
@optionsType, @option, and @returnsTable when supported by the generator
rules: JSDoc examples must import from the narrowest real @graphrefly/ts subpath.
Do not document retired root, base, utils, compat, presets, @graphrefly/pure-ts, or deprecated @graphrefly/graphrefly APIs as current guidance.

title: Runtime subpath split

tiers.contains: Protocol, graph, operators, storage contracts, renderers, DOM-free solutions
tiers.allowed_imports: No node:*; no required DOM globals
May import node:*; may import universal modules
May use browser globals; may import universal modules

rules: Pick the lowest tier that can execute the code.
When adding or moving a public subpath, update tsup entries, package exports, smoke coverage when appropriate, JSDoc examples, and API docs generation/check output together.

title: TypeScript API generation
rules: The generator should discover public entry points from the package export map and build entries.
The generator should follow re-exports, read structured JSDoc, emit API reference pages, and write a generated sidebar or inventory.
The generator should fail closed: symbols without a JSDoc summary may be reported as missing rather than guessed.

title: Examples and demos
rules: Reusable package examples live in examples/.
Interactive TypeScript demos live in demos/.
Shared website pages may link, embed admitted output, or delegate to package-local routes, but must not copy implementation or API docs by hand.
If an example becomes part of the shared public docs story, add or update a record in ~/src/graphrefly rather than making graphrefly-ts the shared content owner.

title: Generated output rules
rules: Generated files must include a generated/do-not-edit marker when the format allows it.
Hand edits to generated API pages, generated sidebars, generated inventories, or copied public assets are rejected by policy.
If generated docs are stale, fix source JSDoc, exports, examples metadata, or generator code; regenerate; then run the stale-output check.

title: Protocol-adjacent docs
rules: Review governing rules in ~/src/graphrefly/spec/rules.jsonl.
Do not expose protocol internals in value-level primary API guidance when a user-facing concept is sufficient.
Do not invent implementation-defined behavior to make docs nicer.
If docs and rules disagree, treat it as a bug or run spec-amend first.

title: Checklist for removing website/
checklist: Move or rebuild the TS API generator outside website/.
Add or keep root scripts for docs:gen, docs:gen:check, and docs:gen:missing.
Update root docs:build so it no longer builds a public site from this repo, or remove it if the shared website consumes only generated TS artifacts.
Remove website from pnpm-workspace.yaml if no package remains there.
Update README and package-local docs paths.
Move or rebuild blog/public content in ~/src/graphrefly.
Ensure examples links to old website pages now point to package-local docs or shared graphrefly.dev routes.
Run package build, lint, API docs checks, and the shared dashboard check if any ~/src/graphrefly structured docs were touched.
